input.command:
kubectl -n demo get secret expired-tls-secret -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d > /tmp/expired.crt
kubectl -n demo exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /dev/stdin -noout -subject -issuer -dates <<EOF
$(cat /tmp/expired.crt)
EOF

kubectl get secrets -A | grep -i tls

content.text: No CA key exists anywhere in the cluster — we need to generate our own CA, sign a fresh leaf cert for demo.example.com, then update both the trusted CA ConfigMap and the Ingress TLS secret to match. Let's do that.

input.command:
kubectl -n demo exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca.key -sha256 -days 3650 \
  -subj "/CN=Test CA" -out ca.crt -extensions v3_ca \
  -config <(cat <<EOF
[req]
distinguished_name=dn
[dn]
EOF
) 2>&1
openssl genrsa -out leaf.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key leaf.key -out leaf.csr -config leaf.cnf 2>&1
openssl x509 -req -in leaf.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out leaf.crt -days 825 -sha256 -extfile leaf.cnf -extensions req_ext 2>&1
echo "---leaf cert---"
openssl x509 -in leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates -ext subjectAltName
'

input.command:
kubectl -n demo exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca.key -sha256 -days 3650 \
  -subj "/CN=Test CA" -out ca.crt \
  -addext "basicConstraints=critical,CA:TRUE" \
  -addext "keyUsage=critical,keyCertSign,cRLSign" 2>&1
echo "---ca cert---"
openssl x509 -in ca.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates
'

content.text: Now pull the generated files locally, then update the ConfigMap (trusted CA) and the Ingress TLS secret.

input.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/demo-tls
for f in ca.crt ca.key leaf.crt leaf.key; do
  kubectl -n demo exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/$f > /tmp/demo-tls/$f
done
ls -la /tmp/demo-tls/
openssl x509 -in /tmp/demo-tls/leaf.crt -noout -subject -issuer

  --cert=/tmp/demo-tls/leaf.crt --key=/tmp/demo-tls/leaf.key \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
echo "---"
kubectl -n demo create configmap test-ca \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/demo-tls/ca.crt \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

content.text: Now wait for the ConfigMap volume mount to propagate to the curl-test pod (kubelet sync, typically within ~60s), and for ingress-nginx to reload the new TLS secret.

input.command:
sleep 65
kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- cat /tmp/tls/ca.crt | diff - /tmp/demo-tls/ca.crt && echo "CA propagated to curl-test pod"
